response.justification: While Bogdan demonstrates strong software craftsmanship fundamentals and broad engineering experience, his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirements. Java is listed in skills but is conspicuously absent from nearly every role description, with his actual stack being TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. There is no demonstrated experience with the Spring ecosystem, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise frameworks, and no evidence of JVM internals knowledge. His only IDE-adjacent work was writing C/C++ components for a C-based IDE at National Instruments — a tangential data point that does not translate to JVM IDE development expertise.
strengths: Consistent track record of software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, pairing) across multiple employers
Experience with developer tooling and framework-building, including MCP code generators and agentic workflow infrastructure at re:cinq
Demonstrated ability to work on high-throughput, complex distributed systems (millions of HTTP req/s at Dunnhumby)
concerns: No practical Java or Kotlin depth despite listing them in skills — actual engineering work is overwhelmingly TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang
Zero evidence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework experience across 15+ years of roles
IDE internals experience is limited to C/C++ component work on a C-based IDE (NI LabWindows/CVI), not JVM-based IDE development
